fields.description:
## Why

With the gated server surface in place (this epic's server-scaffolding story, on which this
`depends_on`), the per-ticket audit page must let a human answer, fast: "did this ticket pass its
gates, and if not why — and can I trust how the machine decided that?" The primary users are BOTH a
spot-checking auditor (wants verdict + real problems at a glance) and a gate-debugger (wants full
per-finding provenance). The design is therefore verdict-first, findings-second, provenance-on-demand.

## What

A read-only, single-column, finding-centric audit page (`/ticket/<id>`):

- **Ticket header (compact, collapsible):** title, status, assignee; description + full acceptance
  criteria behind a "details" toggle — NOT a persistent side rail.
- **Gate-status strip:** all three gate verdicts (plan review · completion · code review) shown AT
  ONCE (PASS/FAIL/not-run), so the pass/fail answer needs zero clicks. Detail sections stack below,
  worst-verdict-first.
- **Plan-review and code-review sections** (each with round history):
  - Round navigation as a `◂ round n of N ▸` stepper (newest default), not a dropdown.
  - A per-round **convergence line graph**: x = round number, y = finding count, with four colored
    series (blocking / advisory / dropped / indeterminate) so the auditor sees whether review cycles
    converged (blockers trending down). Series are distinguished by color AND marker shape/line
    style AND direct labels (never color alone).
  - A round headline verdict, and findings **grouped by decision** (Blocking open by default, fully
    legible; Advisory / Dropped / Indeterminate collapsed + counted), sorted by priority within group.
  - **The finding is the atomic unit.** Each finding row expands IN PLACE to its full four-pass
    provenance, read top-to-bottom as one narrative: FIND (pass-1 text, mapped criteria, location,
    evidence, scenarios, impact, suggested fix) → VERIFY (pass-2 binary sub-answers + severity
    attributes) → DECIDE (pass-3 validity × impact = priority, decision, reason) → COACH (pass-4
    move + note). No cross-referencing findings by id across separate panes.
  - **Threshold display:** per finding, show the priority value AND a signed, color-coded
    delta-from-threshold (priority − block_threshold): green when ≥ 0 (finding met/cleared the bar;
    `+0.00` is green), red when < 0. Rendered at two-decimal precision. Labelled as finding strength
    vs the round's block_threshold; the decision chip carries the actual block/advisory/dropped
    outcome (which also reflects `blocking_enabled`).
- **Completion section (criterion-centric, single-pass):** verdict PASS/FAIL, then one row per
  acceptance/success criterion: met/not-met glyph + the criterion + its kind
  (codebase-verifiable / operator-attested) + citation. An operator-attested criterion with no
  recorded attestation is explicitly flagged, not silently passed.
- **Empty/partial states:** each absent gate shows a quiet, text-only "not yet run" panel, never
  hidden.
- **Trust signals** in each round header: gate/model version, timestamp, the round's own
  block_threshold, and signed/certified state if present.

## Approach rationale

Finding-centric IA (finding = row, four passes = its expandable provenance) was chosen over the
pass-as-navigation alternative after an HCD critique: the four passes are a processing pipeline, but
the auditor reasons in findings, and a per-pass layout forces the human to mentally join a finding's
life across four panes (a database join leaking into the UI). Co-locating a finding's find→verify→
decide→coach story answers "why this finding" in one expand and serves both the auditor (grouped,
verdict-first) and the gate-debugger (full provenance) without two separate views. Server-rendered
Jinja2 partials keep the page a pure function of the read-layer data (no client build). Single-column
stacking survives narrow width and 200% zoom (WCAG 1.4.10 reflow).

## Scope

- `src/rebar/audit/server.py` — add the `/ticket/<id>` route consuming the story-E read layer.
- New `src/rebar/audit/templates/*.html` (Jinja2) — page + gate-strip + finding-provenance + panel
  partials; inline CSS; the convergence line graph as inline SVG (no external JS/asset). Each file
  within the module-size policy (< 500 LOC; split partials along real seams, none < 100 LOC).

## Compatibility & rollback

Additive on top of the server-scaffolding story's gated surface; still default-off, loopback-only,
read-only. Rollback = the epic's flag / extra. No data or schema change.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The gate-status strip shows all three gate verdicts (or "not yet run") simultaneously, and a
      FAIL is visible without any click. Oracle: a live end-to-end test on a ticket seeded with a
      passing plan review, a failing completion verdict, and no code review, asserting the rendered
      HTML contains all three states.
- [ ] A finding row expands to its full four-pass provenance (FIND/VERIFY/DECIDE/COACH) in place,
      including the pass-1 evidence, the pass-2 verification, and the pass-3 decision + reason.
      Oracle: an E2E test asserting the expanded finding HTML contains content from each of the four
      passes for one seeded finding.
- [ ] Each finding shows priority plus a signed delta-from-threshold that is green when
      priority ≥ block_threshold (including exactly 0) and red when below, at two-decimal precision.
      Oracle: an E2E/rendering test seeding one over-threshold and one under-threshold finding and
      asserting the sign, value, and color class of each badge.
- [ ] Findings are grouped by decision with Blocking expanded and Advisory/Dropped/Indeterminate
      collapsed-and-counted, sorted by priority within each group. Oracle: an E2E test asserting the
      group order, the blocking rows are expanded, and the collapsed groups show correct counts.
- [ ] The per-round convergence line graph renders one colored, shape-distinguished, labelled series
      each for blocking/advisory/dropped/indeterminate across the retained rounds. Oracle: an E2E
      test on a ticket with ≥2 rounds asserting the SVG contains all four labelled series and a point
      per round.
- [ ] The completion panel renders one row per criterion (met glyph + kind + citation) and flags an
      operator-attested criterion lacking a recorded attestation. Oracle: an E2E test on a mixed
      completion verdict.
- [ ] A ticket with no code review / no completion verdict shows an explicit "not yet run" panel, not
      a blank or error. Oracle: an E2E test on a plan-review-only ticket.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
